featured_meals.heading:
MEALS THAT HIT
DIFFERENT
featured_meals.description: Every dish is made fresh from scratch with organic ingredients, custom macros for your goals, and flavors that remind you of home.

items.name: Szechuan Spicy Chicken
items.description: Tender chicken breast in a fiery Szechuan sauce with stir-fried vegetables and turmeric rice.
items.longDescription: This is one of Jade's most popular dishes and for good reason. Juicy, hand-sliced chicken breast is wok-tossed in a house-made Szechuan sauce that brings real heat without overpowering the flavor. Paired with anti-inflammatory turmeric jasmine rice and a colorful medley of stir-fried zucchini, bell peppers, and snap peas. Every container is portioned to hit your exact macro targets while tasting like something you'd order at your favorite Chinese restaurant.
items.ingredients: Organic chicken breast, Szechuan peppercorns, dried red chilies, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, rice vinegar, turmeric jasmine rice, zucchini, bell peppers, snap peas, sesame oil, scallions
items.cookingSteps:
Hand-slice fresh organic chicken breast into even strips for consistent cooking
Marinate in house-made Szechuan sauce with peppercorns, garlic, and ginger for 2 hours
Wok-toss chicken over high heat until perfectly seared with a slight char
Stir-fry seasonal vegetables separately to keep them crisp and vibrant
Cook jasmine rice with fresh turmeric root for color and anti-inflammatory benefits
Portion everything into glass containers with macros tracked to the gram
items.reheatingTips: Microwave: Remove lid, heat 2-3 minutes. Stovetop: Toss in a hot pan with a splash of water for 4-5 minutes. Best enjoyed within 24 hours of reheating.

items.name: Hong Shao Rou
items.description: Traditional red braised pork belly, slow-cooked to perfection with star anise, ginger, and soy.
items.longDescription: Hong Shao Rou is the ultimate Chinese comfort food — rich, savory red-braised pork belly that melts in your mouth. Jade slow-cooks this for over 3 hours using her family's recipe passed down through generations. The sauce is a perfect balance of dark soy, rock sugar, Shaoxing wine, and warm spices. Served with steamed bok choy and fluffy white rice to soak up every drop of that legendary braising liquid. This is the dish that started it all.
items.ingredients: Pork belly, dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, rock sugar, star anise, cinnamon stick, ginger, scallions, bay leaves, dried tangerine peel, steamed bok choy, jasmine rice
items.cookingSteps:
Select thick-cut pork belly and blanch to remove impurities
Sear each piece in a hot wok until golden on all sides
Build the braising liquid with dark soy, rock sugar, Shaoxing wine, and whole spices
Slow-braise for 3+ hours on low heat until the meat is fork-tender and glazed
Steam fresh bok choy and cook jasmine rice to pair
Portion into glass containers — the sauce thickens beautifully when refrigerated
items.reheatingTips: Microwave: Heat 3-4 minutes with lid cracked. Stovetop (recommended): Warm gently in a small pot over medium-low heat for 5-6 minutes — the sauce re-glazes perfectly. Do not overheat or the pork will dry out.

items.name: Teriyaki Chicken Bowl
items.description: Lean chicken breast with house teriyaki glaze, garlic asparagus, and fluffy jasmine rice.

items.name: Mapo Tofu Power Bowl
items.description: Spicy Sichuan-style tofu with 93% lean ground beef, chili oil, and steamed vegetables.

items.name: Chili Oil Dumplings
items.description: Handmade dumplings in house chili oil with fresh ginger, scallions, and crispy garlic.
items.longDescription: These are not your freezer-aisle dumplings. Every single one is hand-folded by Jade using a thin, delicate wrapper and a savory filling of seasoned pork and chives. The star of the show is the house chili oil — a slow-infused blend of Sichuan peppercorns, dried red chilies, garlic, and sesame that Jade makes fresh every week. Drizzled generously over the dumplings and topped with crispy garlic chips, fresh scallions, and a splash of black vinegar. It's the dish that gets the most DMs asking for the recipe (which she'll never share).
items.ingredients: Organic ground pork, Chinese chives, ginger, garlic, dumpling wrappers, house chili oil, Sichuan peppercorns, dried red chilies, sesame oil, black vinegar, crispy garlic chips, scallions, soy sauce
items.cookingSteps:
Mix seasoned organic ground pork with fresh Chinese chives, minced ginger, and garlic
Hand-fold each dumpling individually using thin wheat wrappers — no machines, no shortcuts
Boil dumplings in small batches until they float and the wrappers turn translucent
Prepare the house chili oil: slow-infuse peppercorns and dried chilies in hot sesame oil
Plate dumplings and drizzle generously with chili oil, black vinegar, crispy garlic chips, and fresh scallions
Portion into glass containers with extra chili oil on the side
items.reheatingTips: Microwave: Heat 2 minutes with a damp paper towel over the top to keep wrappers soft. Stovetop: Pan-fry in a non-stick pan with a splash of oil for 3-4 minutes for crispy bottoms (highly recommended). Steam: Place in a steamer basket for 4-5 minutes.

items.name: Wild Salmon & Greens
items.description: Wild-caught Alaska sockeye salmon with organic broccoli, squash, and lemon herb sauce.

about.heading: HI, I'M JADE
paragraphs: I grew up watching my family cook incredible Chinese food from scratch — recipes passed down through generations. When I started my fitness journey, I realized that most meal prep services were bland, boring, and full of shortcuts.

So I started cooking for friends. Then friends of friends. Then strangers who became friends. Every meal I make comes from the same place: a genuine love for food and a deep care for what you put in your body.
I'm not a corporation. I'm the person who shops for your organic broccoli at 6am, hand-folds your dumplings, and tracks your macros because I want you to hit your goals as badly as you do.

items.text: Honestly the best meal prep I've ever had. The flavors are restaurant-quality but with wholesome, simple ingredients. Jade genuinely cares about what she's putting in your food.
items.author: Happy Customer

items.text: I've tried so many meal prep companies and none of them come close. The Szechuan chicken and dumplings are on another level. My gym gains have never been better.
items.author: Fitness Enthusiast

items.text: First order and I'm already hooked. The glass containers, the macro tracking, the actual flavor — everything about this is a cut above. Highly recommend.
items.author: New Customer
